content.text: Tôi sẽ đọc spec và lấy thông tin PR #51 song song.

content.content:
1→# Message Formatting Design Spec 2→ 3→**Date:** 2026-03-24 4→**Status:** Approved 5→ 6→## Overview 7→ 8→Unified message formatting system for all OpenACP adapters. Introduces a 2-layer architecture: a shared formatter that converts `OutgoingMessage` into a platform-agnostic `FormattedMessage`, and per-adapter renderers that convert to native output (Telegram HTML, Discord markdown, Slack mrkdwn, Web React components). Includes smart tool call summaries with expandable detail. 9→ 10→## Decisions 11→ 12→| Aspect | Decision | 13→|---|---| 14→| Scope | All message types, all adapters | 15→| Target adapters | Telegram + Discord + Web chat (Slack future-ready) | 16→| Tool display | Smart 1-line summary + click to expand detail | 17→| Architecture | Shared formatter → adapter renderer | 18→| Shared utils | Extract duplicated code from Telegram/Discord | 19→ 20→## Architecture 21→ 22→### 2-Layer Design 23→ 24→``` 25→Layer 1: Shared Formatter (platform-agnostic) 26→ OutgoingMessage → FormattedMessage (structured data) 27→ 28→Layer 2: Adapter Renderer (platform-specific) 29→ FormattedMessage → native output 30→ Telegram: HTML tags (, , 
)
    31→    Discord:  Markdown (**bold**, ```code```)
    32→    Slack:    mrkdwn (*bold*, ```code```)
    33→    Web:      React component props
    34→```
    35→
    36→**Pipeline position:** The formatter sits AFTER `MessageTransformer` (which converts `AgentEvent` → `OutgoingMessage`) and BEFORE adapter rendering. It replaces the per-adapter formatting logic inside `MessageHandlers.onToolCall()`, `onPlan()`, etc.
    37→
    38→```
    39→AgentEvent → MessageTransformer → OutgoingMessage → SharedFormatter → FormattedMessage → AdapterRenderer → native output
    40→```
    41→
    42→`MessageTransformer` continues to handle the `AgentEvent` → `OutgoingMessage` conversion (extracting text, metadata, attachments). The shared formatter takes `OutgoingMessage` and produces `FormattedMessage` with smart summaries and structured metadata.
    43→
    44→**Required MessageTransformer change:** Add `rawInput: event.rawInput` to metadata in `MessageTransformer.transform()` for `tool_call` and `tool_update` cases. Without rawInput, `formatToolSummary()` cannot extract tool args (file_path, command, pattern) and would always fallback to generic `🔧 ToolName`. This is a 1-line change per case in `message-transformer.ts`.
    45→
    46→### FormattedMessage Interface
    47→
    48→```typescript
    49→interface FormattedMessage {
    50→  summary: string          // 1-line human-readable summary
    51→  detail?: string          // Full content for expanded view
    52→  icon: string             // Status/type emoji
    53→  originalType: string     // Preserves OutgoingMessage.type (e.g. "tool_call" vs "tool_update")
    54→  style: MessageStyle      // Grouped style for rendering decisions
    55→  metadata?: MessageMetadata
    56→}
    57→
    58→type MessageStyle = "text" | "thought" | "tool" | "plan" | "usage" | "system" | "error" | "attachment"
    59→// Note: style groups related types (tool_call + tool_update → "tool", session_end + system_message → "system").
    60→// Renderers that need to distinguish (e.g. tool_call = new message, tool_update = edit existing) use originalType.
    61→
    62→interface MessageMetadata {
    63→  toolName?: string
    64→  toolStatus?: "running" | "done" | "error"
    65→  toolKind?: string         // read, edit, write, bash, grep, etc.
    66→  filePath?: string
    67→  command?: string
    68→  planEntries?: { content: string; status: string }[]
    69→  tokens?: number
    70→  contextSize?: number
    71→  cost?: number
    72→  viewerLinks?: { type: "file" | "diff"; url: string; label: string }[]
    73→}
    74→```
    75→
    76→## Smart Summary Logic
    77→
    78→### Tool Call Summaries
    79→
    80→The shared formatter generates 1-line summaries by pattern-matching on tool name and extracting relevant args from the ACP content structure.
    81→
    82→| Tool | Args extracted | Summary example |
    83→|---|---|---|
    84→| `Read` | `file_path`, `limit` | `📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines)` |
    85→| `Edit` | `file_path` | `✏️ Edit src/app.ts` |
    86→| `Write` | `file_path` | `📝 Write new-file.ts` |
    87→| `Bash` | `command` (first 60 chars) | `▶️ Run: pnpm test` |
    88→| `Grep` | `pattern`, `path` | `🔍 Grep "TODO" in src/` |
    89→| `Glob` | `pattern` | `🔍 Glob **/*.ts` |
    90→| `Agent` | `description` | `🧠 Agent: Search codebase` |
    91→| `WebFetch` | URL | `🌐 Fetch https://api.example.com` |
    92→| `WebSearch` | query | `🌐 Search "react markdown"` |
    93→| Unknown | — | `🔧 ToolName` |
    94→
    95→**Implementation:** `formatToolSummary(name: string, content: unknown): string`
    96→- Parse `content` (may be string, object, or nested ACP content block)
    97→- Use `extractContentText()` (existing shared util) for nested ACP content blocks
    98→- Match tool name case-insensitively against known patterns
    99→- Fallback to `🔧 {name}` for unknown tools
   100→
   101→### Status Icons
   102→
   103→Shared constants:
   104→
   105→```typescript
   106→const STATUS_ICONS: Record = {
   107→  pending: "⏳",
   108→  in_progress: "🔄",
   109→  completed: "✅",
   110→  failed: "❌",
   111→  cancelled: "🚫",
   112→  // Aliases for compatibility
   113→  running: "🔄",
   114→  done: "✅",
   115→  error: "❌",
   116→}
   117→
   118→const KIND_ICONS: Record = {
   119→  read: "📖",
   120→  edit: "✏️",
   121→  write: "✏️",             // alias for edit
   122→  delete: "🗑️",
   123→  execute: "▶️",
   124→  command: "▶️",            // normalized from Discord's ⚡ (intentional change for consistency)
   125→  bash: "▶️",              // alias for execute
   126→  search: "🔍",
   127→  web: "🌐",
   128→  fetch: "🌐",             // alias for web
   129→  agent: "🧠",
   130→  think: "🧠",             // alias for agent
   131→  install: "📦",
   132→  move: "📦",              // alias for install
   133→  other: "🛠️",             // fallback for unknown kinds
   134→}
   135→// Canonical superset mapping — includes all keys from both Telegram and Discord adapters plus aliases.
   136→// Note: Discord currently uses ⚡ for "command" — this normalizes to ▶️ for cross-adapter consistency.
   137→```
   138→
   139→## Message Type Formatting
   140→
   141→### Format Specification per Type
   142→
   143→| Type | Icon | Summary | Detail | Collapsible | Style |
   144→|---|---|---|---|---|---|
   145→| `text` | — | Full markdown text | — | No | `text` |
   146→| `thought` | 💭 | First 80 chars + "..." | Full thought text | Yes | `thought` |
   147→| `tool_call` | Smart (kind-based) | Smart summary from args | Tool args + output content | Yes | `tool` |
   148→| `tool_update` | Smart (kind-based) | Smart summary + status icon | Updated output content | Yes | `tool` |
   149→| `plan` | 📋 | "Plan: {N} steps" | Checklist: status icon + content per entry | Yes | `plan` |
   150→| `usage` | 📊 | "{tokens} tokens · ${cost}" | Full breakdown (tokens, context, cost, progress bar) | No | `usage` |
   151→| `error` | ❌ | Error message (first 120 chars) | Full error + stack trace | Yes | `error` |
   152→| `session_end` | ✅ or ❌ | "Session {reason}" | — | No | `system` |
   153→| `system_message` | ℹ️ | Message text | — | No | `system` |
   154→| `attachment` | 📎 | File name + type | — | No | `attachment` |
   155→
   156→**Note:** `permission_request` is NOT an `AgentEvent` type — it flows through a separate `PermissionRequest` interface in `session-bridge.ts`. Permission formatting is handled independently by each adapter's `sendPermissionRequest()`, not by this formatter.
   157→
   158→**Skipped events** (passed through without formatting):
   159→- `image_content` / `audio_content` — handled by `FileService` and adapter-specific media rendering
   160→- `commands_update` — UI-only event for skill command buttons, not user-visible content
   161→
   162→### Formatter Function
   163→
   164→```typescript
   165→function formatOutgoingMessage(msg: OutgoingMessage): FormattedMessage
   166→```
   167→
   168→Central dispatch: switch on `msg.type`, construct `FormattedMessage` with appropriate summary, detail, icon, style, and metadata. Extracts tool args from `msg.metadata` for smart summaries.
   169→
   170→## Adapter Renderers
   171→
   172→Each adapter implements a renderer that converts `FormattedMessage` to native output.
   173→
   174→### Renderer Interface
   175→
   176→```typescript
   177→interface MessageRenderer {
   178→  render(msg: FormattedMessage, expanded: boolean): T
   179→  renderFull(msg: FormattedMessage): T  // non-collapsible messages (text, usage, system)
   180→}
   181→// Telegram/Discord/Slack: MessageRenderer — expanded param ignored (always collapsed)
   182→// Web: MessageRenderer — expanded toggles detail visibility
   183→```
   184→
   185→### Telegram Renderer
   186→
   187→- `render(msg, expanded)` → `{icon} {summary}` (expanded param ignored — Telegram cannot expand inline)
   188→- `renderFull()` → HTML formatted text
   189→- Markdown → HTML conversion (existing `formatMarkdown()`)
   190→- Max length: 3800 chars, smart split at paragraph boundaries
   191→- Viewer links: `📄 file`
   192→
   193→### Discord Renderer
   194→
   195→- `render(msg, expanded)` → `{icon} **{summary}**` (expanded param ignored — Discord cannot expand inline)
   196→- `renderFull()` → native markdown
   197→- Max length: 1800 chars, smart split
   198→- Viewer links: `📄 [file](url)`
   199→
   200→### Slack Renderer (future-ready)
   201→
   202→- `render(msg, expanded)` → `{icon} *{summary}*` (expanded param ignored — Slack uses thread replies for detail)
   203→- `renderFull()` → Slack mrkdwn
   204→- Max length: 3000 chars per block
   205→- Viewer links: `📄 `
   206→
   207→### Web Renderer
   208→
   209→- Does NOT produce strings — returns structured props for React components
   210→- `FormattedMessage` consumed directly by chat components (`ToolCallCard`, `ThoughtCard`, etc.)
   211→- Collapsible: `
/` or custom expand/collapse state
   212→- Code in detail: syntax highlighted via `react-syntax-highlighter`
   213→- Links: native `` tags
   214→
   215→## File Structure
   216→
   217→### New Files
   218→
   219→```
   220→src/adapters/shared/
   221→  message-formatter.ts       — formatOutgoingMessage(), formatToolSummary(), content extraction
   222→  message-formatter.test.ts  — Unit tests for all message types + tool summaries
   223→  format-types.ts            — FormattedMessage, MessageRenderer, icon constants (types + constants only)
   224→  format-utils.ts            — progressBar(), formatTokens(), splitMessage() (shared utilities)
   225→```
   226→
   227→### Modified Files
   228→
   229→```
   230→src/adapters/telegram/formatting.ts
   231→  — Import FormattedMessage, implement TelegramRenderer
   232→  — Refactor existing format functions to use shared formatter
   233→  — Remove duplicated extractContentText(), icon maps
   234→
   235→src/adapters/discord/formatting.ts
   236→  — Import FormattedMessage, implement DiscordRenderer
   237→  — Refactor existing format functions to use shared formatter
   238→  — Remove duplicated extractContentText(), icon maps
   239→
   240→src/adapters/telegram/adapter.ts
   241→  — Update message dispatch to use formatter → renderer pipeline
   242→
   243→src/adapters/discord/adapter.ts
   244→  — Update message dispatch to use formatter → renderer pipeline
   245→```
   246→
   247→### Web Chat Files (Phase 2 — from UI chat spec, not implemented in this spec)
   248→
   249→```
   250→ui/src/lib/message-renderer.ts  — Web renderer (FormattedMessage → React props)
   251→ui/src/components/chat/
   252→  ToolCallCard.tsx   — Consumes FormattedMessage with collapsible detail
   253→  ThoughtCard.tsx    — Consumes FormattedMessage with collapsible detail
   254→  PlanCard.tsx       — Renders plan entries from metadata
   255→  UsageBlock.tsx     — Renders usage from metadata
   256→```
   257→
   258→Note: Web adapter implementation is a separate phase. This spec establishes the `MessageRenderer` interface and `FormattedMessage` structure that the web adapter will consume. Implementation lives in the UI chat spec.
   259→
   260→## Shared Utilities Extraction
   261→
   262→Currently duplicated between Telegram and Discord formatters. Move to shared:
   263→
   264→| Utility | Current location | New location |
   265→|---|---|---|
   266→| `extractContentText()` | Both formatters | `message-formatter.ts` |
   267→| `progressBar()` | Both formatters | `format-utils.ts` |
   268→| `formatTokens()` | Both formatters | `format-utils.ts` |
   269→| `splitMessage()` | Both formatters | `format-utils.ts` |
   270→| `truncateContent()` | Both formatters | `format-utils.ts` |
   271→
   272→**Note on `truncateContent`:** Max length differs per adapter (Telegram: 3800, Discord: 500). Shared function signature: `truncateContent(text: string, maxLen: number): string` — no default value, adapters must pass explicit limit.
   273→| Status icon map | Both formatters | `format-types.ts` (constants only) |
   274→| Kind icon map | Both formatters | `format-types.ts` (constants only) |
   275→
   276→## Integration with Message Dispatcher
   277→
   278→Current flow:
   279→```
   280→dispatcher.onToolCall(ctx, event) → adapter formats internally
   281→```
   282→
   283→New flow:
   284→```
   285→dispatcher.onToolCall(ctx, msg)
   286→  → const formatted = formatOutgoingMessage(msg)
   287→  → const output = renderer.render(formatted, false)
   288→  → adapter.sendMessage(ctx, output)
   289→```
   290→
   291→The `message-dispatcher.ts` interface stays the same — adapters still implement `MessageHandlers`. The change is internal to each handler: call shared formatter first, then adapter-specific renderer.
   292→
   293→## Expand/Collapse Behavior per Adapter
   294→
   295→| Adapter | Collapsed | Expand trigger | Expanded |
   296→|---|---|---|---|
   297→| **Telegram** | Short summary message | Not expandable (Telegram limitation — no interactive expand). Detail shown in separate reply or on tool completion. | Full message on `tool_update` with status `done` |
   298→| **Discord** | Short summary message | Not expandable natively. Detail appended on `tool_update`. | Full message edited on completion |
   299→| **Slack** | Short summary in blocks | Block Kit `overflow` menu or separate thread reply | Thread reply with detail |
   300→| **Web** | `` summary | Click to expand (CSS transition) | Inline expanded detail with syntax highlighting |
   301→
   302→**Note:** Telegram and Discord don't support interactive expand/collapse in messages. For these adapters:
   303→- `tool_call` with status `running` → show collapsed summary
   304→- `tool_update` with status `done` → edit message to include detail (or append if edit not possible)
   305→- Web chat is the only adapter with true expand/collapse UX
   306→
   307→## Telegram-specific: `formatUsageReport()`
   308→
   309→`telegram/formatting.ts` has a `formatUsageReport()` function (~30 lines) used by the `/usage` command for period summaries with budget tracking. This function stays Telegram-specific for now — it is not part of the shared formatter because it formats aggregated usage data, not individual agent events. Discord and other adapters can implement their own version when needed.
   310→
   311→## Testing Strategy
   312→
   313→### Unit Tests (`message-formatter.test.ts`)
   314→
   315→- **Tool summary tests:** Verify correct summary for each known tool (Read, Edit, Bash, Grep, Write, Agent, etc.)
   316→- **Unknown tool fallback:** Verify `🔧 ToolName` format
   317→- **Content extraction:** Nested ACP content blocks parsed correctly
   318→- **All message types:** Each OutgoingMessage type produces correct FormattedMessage
   319→- **Icon selection:** Status and kind icons mapped correctly
   320→- **Edge cases:** Empty content, missing args, very long content truncation
   321→
   322→### Adapter Renderer Tests
   323→
   324→- `telegram/formatting.test.ts` — Telegram renderer produces valid HTML (no unclosed tags)
   325→- `discord/formatting.test.ts` — Discord renderer respects 1800 char limit
   326→- Both: `splitMessage()` preserves code block integrity (odd backtick detection)
   327→- Note: Both adapters currently have **no formatter tests** — this is an opportunity to add coverage.
   328→
   329→## Implementation Order
   330→
   331→```
   332→1. Extract shared utilities → format-utils.ts, format-types.ts (+ tests)
   333→2. Add rawInput to MessageTransformer metadata (1-line × 2 cases)
   334→3. Implement formatOutgoingMessage() + formatToolSummary() (+ tests)
   335→4. Refactor Discord adapter (simpler, fewer quirks — validate shared formatter works)
   336→5. Refactor Telegram adapter (complex: HTML conversion, viewer links)
   337→6. (Future phase) Web adapter — separate spec/plan cycle
   338→```
   339→
   340→Discord before Telegram because Discord formatting is simpler — validates the shared formatter works correctly before tackling Telegram's HTML conversion.
   341→
   342→## Backward Compatibility
   343→
   344→- **No breaking changes** to adapter interfaces — `MessageHandlers` stays the same
   345→- **Internal refactor only** — formatting logic changes inside adapters
   346→- **Existing message output** may change visually (improved formatting) but no functional regression
   347→- **Gradual adoption** — adapters can migrate one at a time; old formatting still works until replaced
   348→
   349→## Out of Scope (Future)
   350→
   351→- Interactive expand/collapse for Telegram/Discord (platform limitation)
   352→- Rich diff viewer in tool detail (separate feature)
   353→- Tool-specific custom renderers (beyond smart summary)
   354→- Inline image/audio rendering in formatted messages
   355→- User-configurable formatting preferences
   356→

file.content:
# Message Formatting Design Spec **Date:** 2026-03-24 **Status:** Approved ## Overview Unified message formatting system for all OpenACP adapters. Introduces a 2-layer architecture: a shared formatter that converts `OutgoingMessage` into a platform-agnostic `FormattedMessage`, and per-adapter renderers that convert to native output (Telegram HTML, Discord markdown, Slack mrkdwn, Web React components). Includes smart tool call summaries with expandable detail. ## Decisions | Aspect | Decision | |---|---| | Scope | All message types, all adapters | | Target adapters | Telegram + Discord + Web chat (Slack future-ready) | | Tool display | Smart 1-line summary + click to expand detail | | Architecture | Shared formatter → adapter renderer | | Shared utils | Extract duplicated code from Telegram/Discord | ## Architecture ### 2-Layer Design ``` Layer 1: Shared Formatter (platform-agnostic) OutgoingMessage → FormattedMessage (structured data) Layer 2: Adapter Renderer (platform-specific) FormattedMessage → native output Telegram: HTML tags (, , 
)
    Discord:  Markdown (**bold**, ```code```)
    Slack:    mrkdwn (*bold*, ```code```)
    Web:      React component props
```

**Pipeline position:** The formatter sits AFTER `MessageTransformer` (which converts `AgentEvent` → `OutgoingMessage`) and BEFORE adapter rendering. It replaces the per-adapter formatting logic inside `MessageHandlers.onToolCall()`, `onPlan()`, etc.

```
AgentEvent → MessageTransformer → OutgoingMessage → SharedFormatter → FormattedMessage → AdapterRenderer → native output
```

`MessageTransformer` continues to handle the `AgentEvent` → `OutgoingMessage` conversion (extracting text, metadata, attachments). The shared formatter takes `OutgoingMessage` and produces `FormattedMessage` with smart summaries and structured metadata.

**Required MessageTransformer change:** Add `rawInput: event.rawInput` to metadata in `MessageTransformer.transform()` for `tool_call` and `tool_update` cases. Without rawInput, `formatToolSummary()` cannot extract tool args (file_path, command, pattern) and would always fallback to generic `🔧 ToolName`. This is a 1-line change per case in `message-transformer.ts`.

### FormattedMessage Interface

```typescript
interface FormattedMessage {
  summary: string          // 1-line human-readable summary
  detail?: string          // Full content for expanded view
  icon: string             // Status/type emoji
  originalType: string     // Preserves OutgoingMessage.type (e.g. "tool_call" vs "tool_update")
  style: MessageStyle      // Grouped style for rendering decisions
  metadata?: MessageMetadata
}

type MessageStyle = "text" | "thought" | "tool" | "plan" | "usage" | "system" | "error" | "attachment"
// Note: style groups related types (tool_call + tool_update → "tool", session_end + system_message → "system").
// Renderers that need to distinguish (e.g. tool_call = new message, tool_update = edit existing) use originalType.

interface MessageMetadata {
  toolName?: string
  toolStatus?: "running" | "done" | "error"
  toolKind?: string         // read, edit, write, bash, grep, etc.
  filePath?: string
  command?: string
  planEntries?: { content: string; status: string }[]
  tokens?: number
  contextSize?: number
  cost?: number
  viewerLinks?: { type: "file" | "diff"; url: string; label: string }[]
}
```

## Smart Summary Logic

### Tool Call Summaries

The shared formatter generates 1-line summaries by pattern-matching on tool name and extracting relevant args from the ACP content structure.

| Tool | Args extracted | Summary example |
|---|---|---|
| `Read` | `file_path`, `limit` | `📖 Read src/main.ts (50 lines)` |
| `Edit` | `file_path` | `✏️ Edit src/app.ts` |
| `Write` | `file_path` | `📝 Write new-file.ts` |
| `Bash` | `command` (first 60 chars) | `▶️ Run: pnpm test` |
| `Grep` | `pattern`, `path` | `🔍 Grep "TODO" in src/` |
| `Glob` | `pattern` | `🔍 Glob **/*.ts` |
| `Agent` | `description` | `🧠 Agent: Search codebase` |
| `WebFetch` | URL | `🌐 Fetch https://api.example.com` |
| `WebSearch` | query | `🌐 Search "react markdown"` |
| Unknown | — | `🔧 ToolName` |

**Implementation:** `formatToolSummary(name: string, content: unknown): string`
- Parse `content` (may be string, object, or nested ACP content block)
- Use `extractContentText()` (existing shared util) for nested ACP content blocks
- Match tool name case-insensitively against known patterns
- Fallback to `🔧 {name}` for unknown tools

### Status Icons

Shared constants:

```typescript
const STATUS_ICONS: Record = {
  pending: "⏳",
  in_progress: "🔄",
  completed: "✅",
  failed: "❌",
  cancelled: "🚫",
  // Aliases for compatibility
  running: "🔄",
  done: "✅",
  error: "❌",
}

const KIND_ICONS: Record = {
  read: "📖",
  edit: "✏️",
  write: "✏️",             // alias for edit
  delete: "🗑️",
  execute: "▶️",
  command: "▶️",            // normalized from Discord's ⚡ (intentional change for consistency)
  bash: "▶️",              // alias for execute
  search: "🔍",
  web: "🌐",
  fetch: "🌐",             // alias for web
  agent: "🧠",
  think: "🧠",             // alias for agent
  install: "📦",
  move: "📦",              // alias for install
  other: "🛠️",             // fallback for unknown kinds
}
// Canonical superset mapping — includes all keys from both Telegram and Discord adapters plus aliases.
// Note: Discord currently uses ⚡ for "command" — this normalizes to ▶️ for cross-adapter consistency.
```

## Message Type Formatting

### Format Specification per Type

| Type | Icon | Summary | Detail | Collapsible | Style |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `text` | — | Full markdown text | — | No | `text` |
| `thought` | 💭 | First 80 chars + "..." | Full thought text | Yes | `thought` |
| `tool_call` | Smart (kind-based) | Smart summary from args | Tool args + output content | Yes | `tool` |
| `tool_update` | Smart (kind-based) | Smart summary + status icon | Updated output content | Yes | `tool` |
| `plan` | 📋 | "Plan: {N} steps" | Checklist: status icon + content per entry | Yes | `plan` |
| `usage` | 📊 | "{tokens} tokens · ${cost}" | Full breakdown (tokens, context, cost, progress bar) | No | `usage` |
| `error` | ❌ | Error message (first 120 chars) | Full error + stack trace | Yes | `error` |
| `session_end` | ✅ or ❌ | "Session {reason}" | — | No | `system` |
| `system_message` | ℹ️ | Message text | — | No | `system` |
| `attachment` | 📎 | File name + type | — | No | `attachment` |

**Note:** `permission_request` is NOT an `AgentEvent` type — it flows through a separate `PermissionRequest` interface in `session-bridge.ts`. Permission formatting is handled independently by each adapter's `sendPermissionRequest()`, not by this formatter.

**Skipped events** (passed through without formatting):
- `image_content` / `audio_content` — handled by `FileService` and adapter-specific media rendering
- `commands_update` — UI-only event for skill command buttons, not user-visible content

### Formatter Function

```typescript
function formatOutgoingMessage(msg: OutgoingMessage): FormattedMessage
```

Central dispatch: switch on `msg.type`, construct `FormattedMessage` with appropriate summary, detail, icon, style, and metadata. Extracts tool args from `msg.metadata` for smart summaries.

## Adapter Renderers

Each adapter implements a renderer that converts `FormattedMessage` to native output.

### Renderer Interface

```typescript
interface MessageRenderer {
  render(msg: FormattedMessage, expanded: boolean): T
  renderFull(msg: FormattedMessage): T  // non-collapsible messages (text, usage, system)
}
// Telegram/Discord/Slack: MessageRenderer — expanded param ignored (always collapsed)
// Web: MessageRenderer — expanded toggles detail visibility
```

### Telegram Renderer

- `render(msg, expanded)` → `{icon} {summary}` (expanded param ignored — Telegram cannot expand inline)
- `renderFull()` → HTML formatted text
- Markdown → HTML conversion (existing `formatMarkdown()`)
- Max length: 3800 chars, smart split at paragraph boundaries
- Viewer links: `📄 file`

### Discord Renderer

- `render(msg, expanded)` → `{icon} **{summary}**` (expanded param ignored — Discord cannot expand inline)
- `renderFull()` → native markdown
- Max length: 1800 chars, smart split
- Viewer links: `📄 [file](url)`

### Slack Renderer (future-ready)

- `render(msg, expanded)` → `{icon} *{summary}*` (expanded param ignored — Slack uses thread replies for detail)
- `renderFull()` → Slack mrkdwn
- Max length: 3000 chars per block
- Viewer links: `📄 `

### Web Renderer

- Does NOT produce strings — returns structured props for React components
- `FormattedMessage` consumed directly by chat components (`ToolCallCard`, `ThoughtCard`, etc.)
- Collapsible: `
/` or custom expand/collapse state
- Code in detail: syntax highlighted via `react-syntax-highlighter`
- Links: native `` tags

## File Structure

### New Files

```
src/adapters/shared/
  message-formatter.ts       — formatOutgoingMessage(), formatToolSummary(), content extraction
  message-formatter.test.ts  — Unit tests for all message types + tool summaries
  format-types.ts            — FormattedMessage, MessageRenderer, icon constants (types + constants only)
  format-utils.ts            — progressBar(), formatTokens(), splitMessage() (shared utilities)
```

### Modified Files

```
src/adapters/telegram/formatting.ts
  — Import FormattedMessage, implement TelegramRenderer
  — Refactor existing format functions to use shared formatter
  — Remove duplicated extractContentText(), icon maps

src/adapters/discord/formatting.ts
  — Import FormattedMessage, implement DiscordRenderer
  — Refactor existing format functions to use shared formatter
  — Remove duplicated extractContentText(), icon maps

src/adapters/telegram/adapter.ts
  — Update message dispatch to use formatter → renderer pipeline

src/adapters/discord/adapter.ts
  — Update message dispatch to use formatter → renderer pipeline
```

### Web Chat Files (Phase 2 — from UI chat spec, not implemented in this spec)

```
ui/src/lib/message-renderer.ts  — Web renderer (FormattedMessage → React props)
ui/src/components/chat/
  ToolCallCard.tsx   — Consumes FormattedMessage with collapsible detail
  ThoughtCard.tsx    — Consumes FormattedMessage with collapsible detail
  PlanCard.tsx       — Renders plan entries from metadata
  UsageBlock.tsx     — Renders usage from metadata
```

Note: Web adapter implementation is a separate phase. This spec establishes the `MessageRenderer` interface and `FormattedMessage` structure that the web adapter will consume. Implementation lives in the UI chat spec.

## Shared Utilities Extraction

Currently duplicated between Telegram and Discord formatters. Move to shared:

| Utility | Current location | New location |
|---|---|---|
| `extractContentText()` | Both formatters | `message-formatter.ts` |
| `progressBar()` | Both formatters | `format-utils.ts` |
| `formatTokens()` | Both formatters | `format-utils.ts` |
| `splitMessage()` | Both formatters | `format-utils.ts` |
| `truncateContent()` | Both formatters | `format-utils.ts` |

**Note on `truncateContent`:** Max length differs per adapter (Telegram: 3800, Discord: 500). Shared function signature: `truncateContent(text: string, maxLen: number): string` — no default value, adapters must pass explicit limit.
| Status icon map | Both formatters | `format-types.ts` (constants only) |
| Kind icon map | Both formatters | `format-types.ts` (constants only) |

## Integration with Message Dispatcher

Current flow:
```
dispatcher.onToolCall(ctx, event) → adapter formats internally
```

New flow:
```
dispatcher.onToolCall(ctx, msg)
  → const formatted = formatOutgoingMessage(msg)
  → const output = renderer.render(formatted, false)
  → adapter.sendMessage(ctx, output)
```

The `message-dispatcher.ts` interface stays the same — adapters still implement `MessageHandlers`. The change is internal to each handler: call shared formatter first, then adapter-specific renderer.

## Expand/Collapse Behavior per Adapter

| Adapter | Collapsed | Expand trigger | Expanded |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Telegram** | Short summary message | Not expandable (Telegram limitation — no interactive expand). Detail shown in separate reply or on tool completion. | Full message on `tool_update` with status `done` |
| **Discord** | Short summary message | Not expandable natively. Detail appended on `tool_update`. | Full message edited on completion |
| **Slack** | Short summary in blocks | Block Kit `overflow` menu or separate thread reply | Thread reply with detail |
| **Web** | `` summary | Click to expand (CSS transition) | Inline expanded detail with syntax highlighting |

**Note:** Telegram and Discord don't support interactive expand/collapse in messages. For these adapters:
- `tool_call` with status `running` → show collapsed summary
- `tool_update` with status `done` → edit message to include detail (or append if edit not possible)
- Web chat is the only adapter with true expand/collapse UX

## Telegram-specific: `formatUsageReport()`

`telegram/formatting.ts` has a `formatUsageReport()` function (~30 lines) used by the `/usage` command for period summaries with budget tracking. This function stays Telegram-specific for now — it is not part of the shared formatter because it formats aggregated usage data, not individual agent events. Discord and other adapters can implement their own version when needed.

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests (`message-formatter.test.ts`)

- **Tool summary tests:** Verify correct summary for each known tool (Read, Edit, Bash, Grep, Write, Agent, etc.)
- **Unknown tool fallback:** Verify `🔧 ToolName` format
- **Content extraction:** Nested ACP content blocks parsed correctly
- **All message types:** Each OutgoingMessage type produces correct FormattedMessage
- **Icon selection:** Status and kind icons mapped correctly
- **Edge cases:** Empty content, missing args, very long content truncation

### Adapter Renderer Tests

- `telegram/formatting.test.ts` — Telegram renderer produces valid HTML (no unclosed tags)
- `discord/formatting.test.ts` — Discord renderer respects 1800 char limit
- Both: `splitMessage()` preserves code block integrity (odd backtick detection)
- Note: Both adapters currently have **no formatter tests** — this is an opportunity to add coverage.

## Implementation Order

```
1. Extract shared utilities → format-utils.ts, format-types.ts (+ tests)
2. Add rawInput to MessageTransformer metadata (1-line × 2 cases)
3. Implement formatOutgoingMessage() + formatToolSummary() (+ tests)
4. Refactor Discord adapter (simpler, fewer quirks — validate shared formatter works)
5. Refactor Telegram adapter (complex: HTML conversion, viewer links)
6. (Future phase) Web adapter — separate spec/plan cycle
```

Discord before Telegram because Discord formatting is simpler — validates the shared formatter works correctly before tackling Telegram's HTML conversion.

## Backward Compatibility

- **No breaking changes** to adapter interfaces — `MessageHandlers` stays the same
- **Internal refactor only** — formatting logic changes inside adapters
- **Existing message output** may change visually (improved formatting) but no functional regression
- **Gradual adoption** — adapters can migrate one at a time; old formatting still works until replaced

## Out of Scope (Future)

- Interactive expand/collapse for Telegram/Discord (platform limitation)
- Rich diff viewer in tool detail (separate feature)
- Tool-specific custom renderers (beyond smart summary)
- Inline image/audio rendering in formatted messages
- User-configurable formatting preferences

## Summary

- Extract duplicated formatting utilities from Telegram/Discord adapters into shared modules (`src/adapters/shared/`)
- Add `FormattedMessage` interface and `MessageRenderer<T>` for platform-agnostic message formatting
- Implement smart tool call summaries (Read → `📖 Read src/main.ts`, Bash → `▶️ Run: pnpm test`, etc.)
- Forward `rawInput` in MessageTransformer tool metadata to enable smart summaries
- Refactor both Telegram and Discord formatters to use shared utils while preserving existing output
- Export `telegramRenderer` and `discordRenderer` implementing `MessageRenderer` interface

- [x] Build succeeds (`pnpm build`)
- [x] 46 new unit tests for shared formatter, tool summaries, and utilities
- [ ] Manual verification: Telegram message formatting unchanged
- [ ] Manual verification: Discord message formatting unchanged

content.text: Build và tests đều pass (1258 tests, 0 fail). Dưới đây là kết quả review chi tiết. --- ## Review PR #51 — Unified Message Formatting ### Tổng quan PR thêm shared formatting layer (`src/adapters/shared/`) với `formatOutgoingMessage()`, `formatToolSummary()`, và utility functions. Refactor cả Telegram và Discord adapter để dùng shared utils. Code chất lượng tốt, tests cover tốt. Tuy nhiên có một số issues cần lưu ý: --- ### 1. Dead code: `DISCORD_KIND_ICONS` không được dùng **File:** `src/adapters/discord/formatting.ts:20-23` ```typescript const DISCORD_KIND_ICONS: Record = { ...KIND_ICONS, command: "⚡", }; ``` Biến này được define nhưng **không có code nào reference nó**. Cả `formatToolCall`, `formatToolUpdate`, và `discordRenderer` đều dùng `STATUS_ICONS` từ shared, không bao giờ dùng `DISCORD_KIND_ICONS`. Nên xóa hoặc wire vào. --- ### 2. Renderers không match spec — thiếu bold formatting **Spec nói:** - Telegram: `{icon} {summary}` - Discord: `{icon} **{summary}**` **Code thực tế:** `telegram/formatting.ts:227`: ```typescript return `${escapeHtml(msg.summary)}${detail}`; // ← no wrapping ``` `discord/formatting.ts:112`: ```typescript return `${msg.summary}${detail}`; // ← no ** wrapping ``` Trong khi `formatToolCall()` (dùng trực tiếp bởi ToolCallTracker) **có** bold: `${si} ${escapeHtml(summary)}`. Tức là 2 code paths cho cùng 1 output nhưng **format khác nhau**. `★ Insight ─────────────────────────────────────` Hiện tại adapters vẫn dùng `formatToolCall()` trực tiếp qua ToolCallTracker, chưa dùng `telegramRenderer`/`discordRenderer`. Nên đây chưa phải bug runtime, nhưng khi migrate sang renderer pipeline thì sẽ bị mất bold. Nên fix renderers ngay để tránh regression sau. `─────────────────────────────────────────────────` --- ### 3. Write tool icon khác spec **Spec:** > `Write` → `📝 Write new-file.ts` **Code** (`message-formatter.ts:58`): ```typescript return fp ? `✏️ Write ${fp}` : `🔧 ${name}`; // ← ✏️ thay vì 📝 ``` Spec dùng `📝` cho Write, `✏️` cho Edit. Code dùng `✏️` cho cả hai. --- ### 4. `formatToolSummary` signature khác spec (nhưng tốt hơn) **Spec:** `formatToolSummary(name: string, content: unknown): string` **Code:** `formatToolSummary(name: string, rawInput: unknown): string` Đây không phải bug — `rawInput` chứa tool input parameters (file_path, command...) nên đúng logic hơn `content`. Nhưng **spec nên update** để reflect code thực tế, tránh confuse. --- ### 5. Hai code paths song song cho tool formatting Hiện tại mỗi adapter có **2 paths** format tool messages: | Path | Entry point | Used by | Bold? | |------|-------------|---------|-------| | Direct | `formatToolCall()` / `formatToolUpdate()` | ToolCallTracker | ✅ Yes | | Renderer | `telegramRenderer.render()` / `discordRenderer.render()` | Not used yet | ❌ No | Cả hai đều exported, nhưng renderers chưa wired vào adapter pipeline. Nếu intent là gradual migration thì OK, nhưng nên comment rõ. Nếu không, renderers nên bị remove để giảm duplication. --- ### 6. `toolStatus` type loosening **Spec:** `toolStatus?: "running" | "done" | "error"` **Code** (`format-types.ts:15`): `toolStatus?: string` Đây là conscious decision vì `STATUS_ICONS` map có nhiều values hơn (pending, in_progress, completed, failed, cancelled). Nhưng spec nên update. --- ### 7. Quote style churn trong diff PR diff cho `discord/adapter.ts` có **hàng trăm dòng** chỉ đổi single quotes → double quotes. Đây là noise lớn cho reviewer. Nên tách formatter changes riêng, style changes riêng, hoặc config linter để tự động handle. --- ### 8. Minor: `stripCodeFences` regex edge case `format-utils.ts:11`: ```typescript return text.replace(/```\w*\n?/g, "").replace(/```$/gm, "").trim(); ``` Regex không handle: - Fences có space trước language: ` ``` typescript ` - Windows line endings `\r\n` - Indented fences (4-space or tab prefix) Đây là edge case nhưng đáng lưu ý nếu agent output có format khác thường. --- ### Tổng kết | Category | Status | |----------|--------| | Tests | ✅ 1258 pass, 46 new tests | | Build | ✅ Clean | | Core logic | ✅ `formatToolSummary`, `extractContentText`, `formatOutgoingMessage` đều đúng | | Backward compat | ✅ Không breaking change | | Spec alignment | ⚠️ 4 deviations (icon, signature, bold, types) | | Dead code | ⚠️ `DISCORD_KIND_ICONS` unused | | Dual code paths | ⚠️ Renderers exist but unused — potential future confusion | **Recommendation:** PR safe to merge cho phase hiện tại (smart summaries work correctly qua direct path). Nên tạo follow-up issues cho: 1. Fix renderer bold formatting 2. Clean up `DISCORD_KIND_ICONS` or wire it in 3. Update spec to match actual code decisions 4. Tách quote style changes thành separate commit
